name: Remove-Module

options.name: -Force
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet removes read-only modules. By default, `Remove-Module` removes only read-write modules.

options.name: -FullyQualifiedName
options.description: The value can be a module name, a full module specification, or a path to a module file.

options.name: -ModuleInfo
options.description: Specifies the module objects to remove. Enter a variable that contains a PSModuleInfo object or a command that gets a module object, such as a `Get-Module` command. You can also pipe module objects to `Remove-Module`.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies the names of modules to remove. Wildcard characters are permitted. You can also pipe name strings to `Remove-Module`.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet isn't run.

description: Removes modules from the current session.
